visa_policy.notes: Visa-free list sourced from Kazakhstan's official Electronic Government Portal (egov.kz). Only ordinary (N) passport holders with unconditional nationality-based visa-free access are listed in visa_free. Countries where only diplomatic/service passports are visa-free are listed exclusively in conditional_access with basis=passport_type. E-visa (109 countries) entry/exit restricted to Astana and Almaty international airports; invitation from a Kazakh host required. No visa on arrival. No ETA system.

outbound_official.notes: Source is Kazakhstan's Electronic Government Portal (egov.kz), page titled 'Visa regime for citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan', last updated 27.04.2026. Visa-free list covers ordinary (N) passport holders with no prior consular visa required. Includes: 30-day visa-free: Egypt, India, Iran (14 days), Jordan, Oman (14 days), Pakistan, Qatar, San Marino, Seychelles, Sierra Leone, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam, Namibia, Nicaragua, Morocco, Malaysia, Maldives, Philippines, Ecuador, Dominican Republic (60 days), Cuba, Barbados (28 days), Brazil, Argentina, China, Colombia. 90-day visa-free: Albania, Andorra, Austria (from May 1 2026), Azerbaijan, Belarus, Bulgaria, Czech Republic, Denmark, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Israel, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Mexico, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro (May–Oct only), Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Russian Federation (requires RUID from July 2026), Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Switzerland, Tunisia, Turkey (90 days in 180), Ukraine. 180-day/1-year: Armenia, Georgia (1 year), Tajikistan, Uzbekistan. Visa on arrival: Mauritius, Zambia. E-visa: Indonesia. ETA/K-ETA: Republic of Korea (30-day stay). Russian Federation requires RUID electronic registration from July 1, 2026.

cbi.notes: Kazakhstan does not offer a Citizenship-by-Investment program. Citizenship is acquired through naturalization after at least 5 years of continuous legal residence; the President of Kazakhstan makes naturalization decisions on a discretionary basis. Kazakhstan explicitly prohibits dual citizenship. Source: invest.gov.kz and egov.kz entry/residence materials.

programs.name: Investor Residence Permit (Golden Visa)
programs.notes: Effective April 30, 2025. Foreign nationals who invest a minimum of USD 300,000 in the charter capital of Kazakh companies or in local publicly traded securities may apply electronically for an investor multiple-entry visa and subsequently obtain a residence permit for up to 10 years. Applications via vmp.gov.kz. Path to citizenship available only through discretionary naturalization after 5 years of continuous legal residency; dual citizenship is not permitted.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Residency (IT Professional Permanent Residence - B9-1)
programs.eligibility: Foreign IT professionals (software development, cybersecurity, UI/UX, DevOps, and related fields) who obtain a petition from the Ministry of Digital Development / Astana Hub confirming qualification; requires IIN and digital signature (EDS) from a Kazakhstan embassy prior to applying
programs.notes: Pilot project launched March 2025. Grants permanent residence permit (duration not explicitly stated on official page). Most of the application process is remote; applicant must buy or rent property in Kazakhstan and provide a medical certificate at final stages. Application processed via eGov.kz after obtaining petition from Ministry of Digital Development / Astana Hub.

programs.name: Neo Nomad Visa (B12-1)
programs.eligibility: Digital professionals and entrepreneurs working remotely for foreign employers; minimum stable monthly income of USD 3,000; requires tax declarations, criminal record clearance, health insurance
programs.notes: Multiple-entry visa valid for up to 1 year; extendable for an additional year from within Kazakhstan. Introduced 2025 by Ministry of Foreign Affairs together with other talent visa categories.

programs.name: Permanent Residence Visa for Skilled Professionals (B9)
programs.eligibility: Highly skilled professionals in in-demand fields: medicine, science, education, innovation, and creative arts
programs.notes: Grants a permanent residence permit (up to 10 years). Introduced 2025 as part of Kazakhstan's talent attraction initiative.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (B12)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay tourism and leisure travel to Kazakhstan. Available both as a standard consular visa and as an electronic visa for eligible nationalities.
visa_types.notes: E-visa available to citizens of 109 countries; e-visa entry/exit restricted to international airports in Astana and Almaty. Standard consular processing up to 5 working days; e-visa issued within 30 minutes. Consular fee: USD 80 single-entry. Multiple-entry version costs USD 200 (1 year). An invitation letter from a Kazakh host is required; facilitated processing without an invitation letter available for citizens of 48 developed countries.

visa_types.name: Business Visa (B1 / B3)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to conduct business activities, negotiations, and commercial dealings in Kazakhstan. B1 covers general business and B3 covers business-related purposes.
visa_types.notes: E-visa available for business purposes to eligible nationalities. Multiple-entry version valid for 1 year at USD 200. Facilitated processing without invitation letter for citizens of 48 countries in B1 and B3 categories. Consular fee: USD 80 single-entry.

visa_types.name: Consulting Services Visa (B2)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to deliver consulting services in Kazakhstan.
visa_types.notes: Separate from general business visa (B1/B3). Requires an invitation from a Kazakh entity. Not listed among e-visa eligible categories; standard consular processing applies.

visa_types.name: Medical Visa (B - Medical)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals traveling to Kazakhstan to receive medical treatment at a Kazakhstan healthcare facility.
visa_types.notes: Explicitly listed as one of the three e-visa types (business, tourist, medical). E-visa entry/exit restricted to Astana and Almaty international airports. Duration is determined by the treatment plan at the time of issuance. Consular fee: USD 80 single-entry.

visa_types.name: Private Visit Visa (B10)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting family members, friends, or private contacts in Kazakhstan. Requires an invitation from the Kazakh-resident host.
visa_types.notes: Facilitated processing without prior invitation available to citizens of 48 countries. Inviting party must register the guest within 3 working days of arrival. Private visit invitations must be submitted between 1 year and 5 working days before arrival. Consular fee: USD 80 single-entry.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit Acquisition Visa (B8)
visa_types.purpose: A transition visa allowing foreign nationals to enter Kazakhstan for the purpose of obtaining a temporary or permanent residence permit.
visa_types.notes: National fee (duty): 7 MCI single-entry (approx. 27,524 KZT at 2025 MCI rate of 3,932 KZT) or 30 MCI multiple-entry (approx. 117,960 KZT). MCI = Monthly Calculation Index set annually by the Government of Kazakhstan.

visa_types.name: Official/Diplomatic Visa (A3)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign government officials, diplomats, and staff of international organisations making official visits to Kazakhstan.
visa_types.notes: Facilitated processing: citizens of 48 eligible countries may obtain A3 visas without an invitation letter. Diplomatic passport holders from many countries already have visa-free access for official travel. Standard processing up to 5 working days; invitation processing up to 3 working days (electronic) or 5 working days (paper).

visa_types.name: Investor Visa (Multiple-Entry)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who invest a minimum of USD 300,000 in the charter capital of Kazakh companies or in locally traded securities, enabling entry and subsequent residence permit acquisition.
visa_types.notes: Effective April 30, 2025. Applications submitted electronically via vmp.gov.kz. After obtaining the investor visa, holder may apply for a residence permit for up to 10 years. Minimum investment: USD 300,000. Multiple-entry consular fee applies (30 MCI / ~USD 200 equivalent).

visa_types.name: Neo Nomad Visa (B12-1)
visa_types.purpose: For digital professionals and entrepreneurs working remotely for foreign employers who wish to reside in Kazakhstan while maintaining employment outside the country.
visa_types.notes: Introduced 2025 by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Valid for up to 1 year, extendable for an additional year from within Kazakhstan. Requirements: minimum stable monthly income of USD 3,000, tax declarations, criminal record clearance, and health insurance. Processing time not published on official pages.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence Visa for Skilled Professionals (B9)
visa_types.purpose: For highly skilled foreign professionals in in-demand fields including medicine, science, education, innovation, and creative arts who seek long-term residence in Kazakhstan.
visa_types.notes: Introduced 2025 as part of Kazakhstan's talent attraction initiative. Grants a permanent residence permit for up to 10 years. Processing time not published on official pages reviewed.

visa_types.name: IT Professional Permanent Residence (B9-1)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign IT professionals (software development, cybersecurity, UI/UX, DevOps, and related fields) to obtain permanent residency in Kazakhstan under a pilot digital nomad scheme.
visa_types.notes: Pilot project launched March 2025. Requires a petition from the Ministry of Digital Development / Astana Hub confirming qualification. Applicant must also obtain an IIN (Individual Identification Number) and digital signature (EDS) from a Kazakhstan embassy prior to applying online via eGov.kz. Must buy or rent property in Kazakhstan and provide a medical certificate at final stages. Processing: 45 calendar days.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa (implicit - not separately coded in public sources)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to transit through Kazakhstan en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Kazakhstan law provides for a transit regime for foreigners passing through its territory. Specific transit visa code not published in accessible official pages; transit visas are issued through Kazakh consulates. Citizens with visa-free access do not require a transit visa. Entry/exit for transit through Astana and Almaty international airports only for e-visa holders.

agreements: EAEU (Eurasian Economic Union) - free movement of labour among Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Russia; citizens do not need separate work permits
CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States) - bilateral visa-free agreements with multiple CIS member states facilitating stays of 30–90 days

conditional_access.conditions: Diplomatic and service passport holders of Bolivia are admitted visa-free for up to 90 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Venezuela are admitted visa-free for up to 90 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic passport holders of Egypt are admitted visa-free for up to 90 days. Service (S) and ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available for ordinary passport holders).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Jordan are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa.
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Laos are admitted visa-free for up to 90 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Montenegro are admitted visa-free for up to 90 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Pakistan are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Paraguay are admitted visa-free for up to 90 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Sierra Leone are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Sri Lanka are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Bangladesh are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Cuba are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Rwanda are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Turkmenistan are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of North Macedonia are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Bosnia and Herzegovina are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Guinea-Bissau are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).
Diplomatic and service passport holders of Lebanon are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available).

Diplomatic passport holders of Congo (Republic) are admitted visa-free for up to 30 days. Service (S) and ordinary (N) passport holders require a visa (e-visa available for ordinary passport holders).

CLEANED conditional_access notes: removed internal 'NOTE: discrepancy to verify' placeholders - all discrepancies now resolved against official egov.kz table.
CONFIRMED: Congo (COG) only diplomatic passport is visa-free; service passport is not. Conditional_access entry and e_visa note updated accordingly.
CONFIRMED: RBI min_amount USD 300,000 verified against invest.gov.kz; effective date corrected to April 30, 2025 (not May 10, 2025).
CONFIRMED: Digital Nomad Residency permit duration not explicitly stated on official egov.kz page; note updated to reflect this.

VALIDATED 2026-06-27: Re-fetched egov.kz/cms/en/articles/for_foreigners/visa_regime_for_foreigners (page updated 22.05.2026). All existing visa_free entries confirmed: South Korea (KOR) 30 days confirmed; Malaysia (MYS) 30 days confirmed; Turkey (TUR) 90 days confirmed; Barbados (BRB) 28 days confirmed. No removals from visa_free or e_visa lists warranted. egov.kz e-visa page confirmed 109 countries eligible and airport-only entry restriction still applies. vmp.gov.kz returned empty content. Qatar (QAT) not visible in egov.kz direct page extraction - flagged as gap (see gaps). Jamaica (JAM) correctly in e_visa only. No changes to visa_free, visa_on_arrival, eta, or e_visa lists made this run.

VALIDATED 2026-06-29: Re-fetched egov.kz inbound visa regime table and e-visa page, and wpk.kz/kaz_entry_eng. Targeted check for Cuba (CUB): egov.kz table confirms Cuba N (ordinary) passport = 'visa' — current record correctly places Cuba in e_visa/conditional_access only. Targeted check for Qatar (QAT): egov.kz table again does not show Qatar; wpk.kz (legal aggregator) lists Qatar in bilateral far-abroad visa-free agreements for 30 days — classified as unresolvable gap due to conflict between sources (see gaps). Cuba/Cuba distinction confirmed. egov.kz e-visa list of 109 countries re-confirmed unchanged. mfa.gov.kz returned TLS error. No changes made to visa_free, visa_on_arrival, eta, e_visa, or conditional_access lists this run.
gaps: E-visa max_stay_days not published as a specific number on egov.kz visa-obtaining page; duration is determined by the invitation/category at time of issuance
Processing time for Neo Nomad Visa (B12-1) and B9 skilled professional visa not stated on official pages reviewed
vmp.gov.kz (main visa-migration portal) did not return readable content when fetched directly (both 2026-06-13 and 2026-06-27 runs)

Qatar (QAT) status unresolved: Qatar does not appear in the egov.kz inbound visa regime table in two separate re-fetches (2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29). However, new.wpk.kz/kaz_entry_eng (a Kazakhstan legal aggregator site synthesising current legislative acts) explicitly lists Qatar under bilateral far-abroad visa-free agreements for 30 days. Per safety rule (false visa-free is worst error), Qatar cannot be added to visa_free without direct egov.kz or mfa.gov.kz confirmation. mfa.gov.kz returned TLS error on 2026-06-29 and could not be read. Recommend a direct re-check of the egov.kz table or mfa.gov.kz at next validation run.

No official Kazakhstani government source was found granting visa-free or reduced-visa entry to holders of third-country credentials (US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, residence permits, Green Card, OCI card, etc.); all official sources confirm entry is based solely on nationality/passport type
Digital Nomad Residency (B9-1) permit duration not explicitly stated on official egov.kz page
